mechanism: HMG-CoA reductase inhibitors; block cholesterol synthesis in liver
interactions: Avoid grapefruit juice (CYP3A4); caution with fibrates, niacin, certain antibiotics
monitoring: LFTs at baseline; CK if muscle symptoms; LDL target <70 mg/dL for high-risk

mechanism: Block serotonin reuptake, increasing synaptic serotonin
onset: 4-6 weeks for full effect; early side effects often resolve in 2 weeks
black_box: Increased suicidal ideation in patients <25 — monitor closely first 4 weeks

mechanism: Block angiotensin-converting enzyme; prevent angiotensin I → II; reduce vasoconstriction
contraindications: Pregnancy (teratogenic), bilateral renal artery stenosis, prior angioedema
monitoring: Potassium and creatinine within 1-2 weeks of starting

mechanism: Reduces hepatic glucose production; improves insulin sensitivity; activates AMPK
advantages: weight neutral or modest weight loss
side_effects: GI upset (nausea, diarrhea) — start low, take with food, use XR formulation
vitamin_B12: Long-term use depletes B12; monitor annually

mechanism: Inhibit bacterial cell wall synthesis (beta-lactam ring binds PBPs)
resistance: Beta-lactamase producing bacteria resistant (clavulanate overcomes this)
allergy: PCN allergy reported in 10% but true allergy in <1% after testing; cross-reactivity with cephalosporins ~2%
complete_course: Always finish antibiotics even if feeling better to prevent resistance

mechanism: Inhibit COX-1 and COX-2; reduce prostaglandin synthesis (pain, fever, inflammation)
risks: cardiovascular events (celecoxib lower GI risk but higher CV)
take_with_food: Always take with food or PPI if prolonged use; use lowest dose shortest time
contraindications: Avoid in pregnancy (especially 3rd trimester), eGFR <30, aspirin-exacerbated respiratory disease

mechanism: Bind glucocorticoid receptors; suppress immune system; anti-inflammatory
taper: Never stop abruptly after >2-3 weeks; must taper to prevent adrenal crisis
